hypothesis: If an XGBoost ∆-learning model is trained to predict the difference between GFN1-xTB and DFT (PBE or HSE06) band gaps of MOFs using ST-135, RAC, and APRDF descriptors, then it will achieve lower cross-validated error than both direct descriptor-based models and fine-tuned MOFTransformer/CGCNN baselines because the xTB→DFT residual is systematic and easier to learn than the absolute mapping.
